title: Generics убирают боксинг: List<int> vs ArrayList

cards.prompt: The non-generic ArrayList boxes every int (its slot is object) for 32056 bytes, while the value-specialized List<int> stores ints inline for 4056 bytes (~8x cheaper); pre-sized and warmed for a deterministic GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread delta
cards.code: using System; using System.Collections; using System.Collections.Generic; const int N=1000; var w1=new ArrayList(N); for(int i=0;i<N;i++)w1.Add(i); var w2=new List<int>(N); for(int i=0;i<N;i++)w2.Add(i); long a0=GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread(); var al=new ArrayList(N); for(int i=0;i<N;i++)al.Add(i); long a1=GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread(); var gl=new List<int>(N); for(int i=0;i<N;i++)gl.Add(i); long a2=GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread(); Console.WriteLine($"ArrayList: {a1-a0} List<int>: {a2-a1}");

cards.prompt: Boxing allocates a new object each time: two (object)42 are distinct heap objects (ReferenceEquals False) with equal values (Equals True), and (int)b1 unboxes to 42, printing False True 42
cards.code: using System; int x = 42; object b1 = x; object b2 = x; Console.WriteLine($"{object.ReferenceEquals(b1, b2)} {b1.Equals(b2)} {(int)b1}");

cards.prompt: Each boxed int is a managed heap object (header + value + padding) costing 24 bytes; 1000 boxes allocate 24000 bytes, 24 per box, measured with a deterministic GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread delta
cards.code: using System; const int N=1000; object sink=null; for(int i=0;i<N;i++) sink=(object)i; long a0=GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread(); for(int i=0;i<N;i++) sink=(object)i; long a1=GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread(); Console.WriteLine($"boxes bytes: {a1-a0} per-box: {(a1-a0)/N}");
